NDA Terms
fields.options:
This Non-Disclosure Agreement is hereby incorporated into the existing Employment Agreement between AIonion Capital Market Services Private Limited or its subsidiaries (Aionion) and the undersigned employee.

As an employee, contractor or temporary staff of Aionion, the employee may have access to confidential information including customer information, financial or business information obtained through their association with Aionion.

Confidential information is considered invaluable and sensitive and unauthorized disclosure of the same can cause significant monetary and reputational damages to Aionion. Legal systems have strict provisions in ensuring protection of confidential information in any form. Inappropriate disclosure of Aionion confidential information by an employee may result in the imposition of monetary penalties and/or initiation of legal proceedings.

In consideration of the above, the employee hereby acknowledges and agrees that the:

  1. Employee will not access confidential information for which they have no legitimate need to know and for which they are not the authorized user. This includes accessing their own confidential information without proper access permission.
  2. Employee will not in any way divulge, copy, release, sell, loan, review, alter or destroy any confidential information unless expressly permitted by existing policy except as properly approved in writing by an authorized officer of Aionion within the scope of my association with Aionion.
  3. Employee will not utilize another user's password in order to access any system nor will he/she reveal his/her computer credentials to anyone else for any reason. The Employee accepts responsibility and understands that he/she will be held accountable for all activities occurring under his/her computer credentials.
  4. If the employee observes or has knowledge of unauthorized access or divulgence of confidential information he/she will report it immediately to the reporting manager and/or Human Resource Manager and/or Managing Director.
  5. The employee agrees not to seek personal benefit or permit others to benefit personally by any confidential information that he/she may have access to or that he/she accesses as an unauthorized user.
  6. The employee understands that all information, regardless of the media on which its stored (paper, computer, mobile, videos, recorders, etc.), the system which processes it (computers, voice mail, mobile, faxes, etc.), or the methods by which its moved (electronic mail, face to face conversation, etc.) is the property of Aionion and shall not be used inappropriately or for personal gain.
  7. The employee also understands that Aionion reserves the right to inspect or monitor any company owned, leased, or controlled computer, computer device, network, computer facility, storage device, voice mail or telephone system at any time for any reason and that Aionion may divulge any information found during such inspections or monitoring to any party it deems appropriate. The employee understands that he/she should not consider electronic communications to be either private or secure, nor have an expectation of privacy in anything he/she creates, stores, sends, or receives on the computer and network.
  8. The employee agrees to abide by all Aionion rules and regulations as specified in Aionion policies unless specifically altered by a separate contractual agreement.
  9. The employee understands that his/her failure to comply with this Agreement may result in corrective action which might include, but is not limited to, termination of employment and/or loss of privileges within Aionion as well as potential civil or criminal penalties.

All training materials and handouts given to the trainees are exclusive property of Aionion. No part of these documents can be duplicated or reproduced without the prior written permission of Aionion.

By signing this agreement, the employee acknowledges that Aionion has an active ongoing program to review records and transactions for inappropriate access, and understands that inappropriate access or disclosure of information can result in penalties up to and including termination of employment and/or legal action.

fields.label: Policy Content
fields.options:
1. Introduction

At Aionion, we are committed to fostering a professional and inclusive work environment. This Employee Fraternization Policy is designed to establish clear guidelines regarding personal relationships within the workplace to prevent conflicts of interest, maintain a fair and transparent work environment, and uphold professional standards.

2. Scope

This policy applies to all employees, including full-time, part-time, temporary, and contract workers, as well as interns and volunteers.

3. Definition of Personal Relationships

Personal relationships covered by this policy include, but are not limited to, romantic relationships, dating, familial relationships (including spouses and siblings).

4. Disclosure Requirements

Employees are required to disclose personal relationships if one individual holds a position of authority over the other. However, voluntary disclosure is encouraged to address potential conflicts proactively.

5. Conflict of Interest

a. Reporting Relationships: Employees in a reporting or supervisory relationship with a family member (spouse or sibling) must disclose the relationship to HR. In such cases, arrangements may be made to mitigate any conflicts of interest, such as reassignment of responsibilities or supervision.

b. Professional Decision-Making: Employees involved in a personal relationship should ensure that their personal ties do not compromise their professional judgment, decisions, or actions.

6. Conduct Expectations

All employees are expected to maintain a professional and respectful workplace environment. Personal relationships, including those with spouses and siblings, should not interfere with work responsibilities or create a disruptive atmosphere.

7. Impact on the Workplace

Employees with personal relationships, including spouses and siblings, should be mindful of how their interactions may be perceived by colleagues. Any behavior that disrupts the workplace or creates discomfort for others is not acceptable.

8. Consensual Relationships

While consensual relationships between adults are a personal matter, employees are expected to be aware of the potential impact of their relationships on the workplace.

9. Reporting Violations

Any Employee who become aware of any violations of this policy should report them to HR or another designated authority. Reports will be handled confidentially and investigated promptly.

10. Retaliation Protection

An Employee who make good faith reports under this policy will not face retaliation. Retaliation against any employee for reporting a violation of this policy is strictly prohibited.

11. Penalties for Non-Compliance

Violations of this policy may result in disciplinary actions, ranging from counselling and reassignment of duties to suspension or termination of employment, depending on the severity and recurrence of the violation.

12. Review and Updates

This policy will be periodically reviewed to ensure its effectiveness and relevance. Any updates will be communicated to all employees.
